title: AVP-Telco Cloud Infrastructure Planning & Engineering
raw_description:
Job description

The AVP – Cloud Infrastructure Planning & Engineering leads the strategic planning, design, and engineering of Telco Cloud infrastructure supporting Core Network functions including BNG, 5G Core (5GC), and any other cloud requirements. This role is critical in ensuring seamless integration and interoperability across vendors (e.g., Nokia, Huawei, and others), particularly in the context of ongoing infrastructure evolution and vendor transition programs. The platform will be built on NFV (OpenStack) and CNF (Kubernetes/container-based architectures), aligned with European Telecommunications Standards Institute (ETSI) standards to ensure consistency, portability, and future scalability. This includes integration with orchestration platforms (e.g., NFV MANO, Kubernetes orchestration) to accelerate service deployment and operational efficiency.

The AVP will lead infrastructure strategy, design and execution, ensuring a smooth migration and coexistence between legacy and next-generation platforms, while maintaining service continuity, performance, and customer experience.

This role will also drive vendor-agnostic architecture, API-driven integration, and automation to minimize vendor lock-in and maximize operational flexibility and cost efficiency (CAPEX & OPEX). 

It requires strong technical leadership and ownership with the ability to independently lead planning, architecture, and execution—leveraging API-driven integration, automation, and AI-based operations to accelerate service delivery and operational excellence.

The position drives cloud modernization, infrastructure governance, capacity planning, redundancy strategy, and cross-functional alignment. It ensures optimal infrastructure lifecycle management, cost-performance efficiency, and adoption of emerging technologies including AI-driven automation and SDN evolution.

In addition, the AVP will work closely with cross-functional teams—including planning, operations, IT, procurement, and vendors—to ensure alignment across all phases of infrastructure lifecycle. The role is responsible for leading the preparation of technical documentation, supporting tender processes (RFP/RFQ), and ensuring proper governance, reporting, and administrative compliance.

Key Responsibilities

Key Responsibilities

Key Result Areas

Lead planning and engineer Telco cloud infrastructure for NFV (OpenStack) and CNF (Kubernetes)

Reliable, scalable, and high-performance cloud platforms

Develop hardware optimization plans for VM and container workloads

Improved utilization and cost efficiency

Lead E2E cloud infrastructure capacity planning and engineering

Accurate forecasts and infrastructure readiness for demand growth

Design Kubernetes and OpenStack cluster architecture

High availability, scalability, and service resilience infrastructure design

Create long-term roadmap and implementation plans for Telco cloud infrastructure

Strategic alignment and modernization readiness

Lead integration planning for network and service orchestration frameworks

Streamlined deployment and automation

Develop multi-scenario hardware strategy including Intel, AMD, and ARM architectures

Platform flexibility and cost-performance optimization

Design hardware roadmap aligned with business and technology evolution

Strategic modernization and lifecycle sustainability

Monitor infrastructure usage and define optimization actions

Improved resource efficiency and budget utilization

Lead redundancy and geo-redundancy planning across cloud deployments

Enhanced network resilience and fault tolerance

Drive AI-based initiatives for cloud infrastructure and SDN operations

Automation, predictive analytics, and operational intelligence

Establish and maintain design principles covering capacity and redundancy planning

Consistent, scalable, and resilient cloud infrastructure design

Oversee integration with orchestration and automation frameworks

Streamlined deployment and operational efficiency

 

Key Responsibility_2

Drive AI-based initiatives for infrastructure and SDN optimization

Predictive analytics and operational intelligence

Establish infrastructure design principles and governance standards

Consistent, scalable, and compliant architecture

Define and implement end-to-end orchestration strategy aligned with ETSI NFV MANO and cloud-native frameworks

· Standardized orchestration framework adopted across NFV and CNF environments

· Reduced service deployment time (e.g., from days to hours/minutes)

· Improved consistency and repeatability of service provisioning

· Seamless integration between infrastructure, network functions, and orchestration platforms

Enable automated provisioning of virtual machines (VMs) and containerized workloads using orchestration tools

· Zero-touch or low-touch provisioning capability for VM and container workloads

· Reduced manual intervention and operational errors

· Faster time-to-market for new services

· Increased infrastructure utilization and efficiency

Integrate infrastructure with orchestration and automation platforms to support end-to-end service lifecycle management

· Full lifecycle automation (Day 0, Day 1, Day 2 operations)

· Improved interoperability across multi-vendor environments

· Streamlined service activation, scaling, and decommissioning

· Reduced dependency on vendor-specific tools (vendor-agnostic architecture)

Drive zero-touch provisioning and closed-loop automation for infrastructure operations
  • Implementation of closed-loop automation with monitoring and auto-remediation
  • Reduced incident resolution time (MTTR improvement)
  • Increased operational efficiency through AI-driven insights
  • Enhanced service reliability and customer experience

Collaborate and align with all relevant stakeholders, including planning, operations, IT, procurement, finance, and external vendors, to ensure end-to-end infrastructure delivery

 

· Strong alignment across cross-functional teams and vendors

· Timely decision-making and issue resolution across departments

· Improved delivery timelines and reduced project delays

  •  High stakeholder satisfaction and effective communication at all levels (technical & executive)

 

Key Responsibility_3

Lead the preparation of technical documentation, support tender processes (RFP/RFQ), and ensure governance and compliance across infrastructure initiatives
  • High-quality and complete technical documentation for RFP/RFQ submissions
  • Successful tender participation with strong technical evaluation outcomes
  • Compliance with internal governance, audit, and regulatory requirements
  • Standardized documentation framework across infrastructure projects

 

Oversee day-to-day coordination, reporting, and administrative activities related to infrastructureplanning and engineering

· Timely and accurate reporting to management and stakeholders

· Well-managed documentation, tracking, and administrative processes

· Improved operational transparency and accountability

· Efficient coordination of ongoing activities and issue tracking

Operate with strong ownership and autonomy in driving planning, decision-making, and execution across infrastructure initiatives

· Effective delivery of initiatives with minimal supervision

· Proactive problem-solving and decision-making

· Strong accountability in achieving project and organizational goals

· Consistent execution aligned with strategic objectives

 

Skill

  • Telco Cloud Infrastructure Architecture
  • NFV (OpenStack) & CNF (Kubernetes) Engineering
  • Cluster Design & High Availability Architecture
  • Capacity Forecasting & Infrastructure Modeling
  • Hardware Architecture (Intel / AMD / ARM)
  • Geo-Redundancy & Disaster Recovery Planning
  • Infrastructure Monitoring & Optimization
  • SDN Integration & Orchestration Frameworks
  • AI-driven Infrastructure Automation
  • Virtualization & Cloud-Native Engineering
  • Cross-Functional & Vendor Collaboration
  • Containerization & Kubernetes ecosystem
  • API architecture & integration (REST, automation frameworks)
  • Infrastructure as Code (IaC) & automation
  • Independent decision-making & execution capability
  • Communication skill and negotiation
  • Project management

Leadership Competency

Competency

Definition

Expected Behaviour

Strategic Thinking

Aligns cloud infrastructure goals with business and technology vision

Develops multi-year infrastructure evolution and roadmap plans aligned with company strategy

Execution Leadership

Drives delivery from planning to implementation

Ensures infrastructure and timely delivery of platform readiness

Collaboration & Influence

Works effectively across functions

Aligns planning, IT, operations, and vendors

Technical Leadership

Provides deep infrastructure expertise

Leads infrastructure strategy and standards adoption

Innovation & Adaptability

Adopts modern and emerging technology in design

Champions AI, automation, and future-ready infrastructure models

Decision Making

Makes informed and data-driven decisions

Balances trade-offs in hardware selection, performance, and cost

Communication & Reporting

Communicates plans and progress effectively

Prepares and delivers updates to stakeholders and executive leadership

 

Qualifications

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or related field

- Minimum 10 years of experience in Telecom infrastructure planning and engineering

- Strong leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills

- Proven expertise in OpenStack and Kubernetes deployment, design, and performance tuning

- Solid understanding of Core Network functions (e.g., BNG, 5G/4G Core, etc)

- Familiarity with ETSI NFV architecture (MANO) and industry standards for multi-vendor environments European Telecommunications 

- Strong knowledge of API-driven architecture and integration (REST APIs, automation frameworks) Standards Institute

- Strong experience in capacity planning, redundancy design, and hardware optimization

- Experience in defining design principles and standardizing infrastructure architecture practices

- Strong understanding of orchestration frameworks and infrastructure automation

- Familiarity with infrastructure hardware platforms across Intel, AMD, and ARM

- Experience in applying AI techniques to cloud or SDN operations

- Excellent communication, leadership, and stakeholder management skills

- Strong experience in vendor management and multi-vendor

- Demonstrated ability to lead initiatives independently, with strong ownership and decision-making capabilities

title: AVP-Fixed Voice Product & Service
raw_description:
Job Summary

The AVP-Fixed Voice Product Management is responsible for driving the strategy, development, lifecycle management, and commercialization of fixed voice services (e.g., SIP Trunking, Hosted PBX, PSTN replacement solutions) for enterprise and SME segments. This role ensures the product portfolio is competitive, scalable, and aligned with market demand, revenue targets, and operational efficiency goals.

Job Details

1.Product Lifecycle Management

  • Own the end-to-end lifecycle of fixed voice products, from launch to retirement.
  • Ensure product relevance through continuous market and customer feedback.
  • Maintain product documentation, feature catalogs, and change logs.

 

2.Operational Excellence

  • Oversee provisioning, activation, and service assurance processes.
  • Monitor SLA compliance and drive continuous improvement initiatives.
  • Collaborate with network and infrastructure teams to ensure deployment readiness.

 

3.Performance & Quality Monitoring

  • Track product performance metrics (revenue achievement, product P&L, etc.)
  • Lead root cause analysis and resolution of recurring service issues.
  • Implement operational dashboards and reporting tools.

 

4.Cross-Functional Coordination

  • Work closely with sales, network, IT, and customer service teams.
  • Ensure alignment between product capabilities and delivery processes.
  • Support pre-sales and post-sales teams with product knowledge and operational insights.

 

5.Commercial Support

  • Provide input to pricing and bundling strategies based on operational feasibility.
  • Support GTM execution with operational readiness and service design.
  • Monitor product profitability and cost-to-serve metrics.

 

6.Team Leadership

  • Lead a lean team of product operations specialists and analysts.
  • Foster a culture of accountability, agility, and customer-centricity.
  • Develop team capabilities in lifecycle and operational management.

 

Minimum Requirements:

 

Education:

  • Bachelor’s degree from a well-known university in Telecommunications, Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, or related STEM field.
  • Master’s degree (MBA or Technology Management) is preferred.
  • Professional certification in voice networking, marketing, or related field is a plus.
  • Recent formal and informal education in Artificial Intelligence and its applications is a plus.

 

Professional Experience:

  • 10-15 years of experience in product management, preferably in fixed voice, connectivity, or telecom solutions (e.g., SIP Trunk, Cloud PBX, LAN, WAN, etc.).
  • Experience in the telecom or ICT industry with exposure to enterprise/B2B markets.
  • Strong background in cross-functional collaboration (technology, sales, delivery, operations).
  • Proven experience leading product lifecycle (development, launch, enhancement, support).
  • Hands-on involvement in product PoC, technical support, and pre-sales activities.

 

Expected Skills

  • Technical & Commercial Understanding with solid grasp of voice network technologies (OSI Layer, fiber optic, SBC, Voice Firewall etc.) and enterprise connectivity solutions.
  • Product Management with ability to define roadmap, manage lifecycle, support sales, and ensure delivery excellence.
  • Analytical Thinking and Reporting with ability to monitor performance, revenue, project pipeline, and churn metrics effectively.

title: Facility Manager Circle Java
raw_description:
Job Description

Responsible for overseeing the daily operations, maintenance, and safety of a building or facility. This job scope includes:
1. Building Operations
•  Ensure the building is fully operational and meets safety, regulatory, and functional standards.
•  Oversee building systems, including HVAC, elevators, plumbing, electrical, and security systems.
•  Manage cleaning and janitorial services to maintain a clean and hygienic environment.
2. Maintenance and Repairs
•  Schedule regular inspections and preventive maintenance for building equipment and infrastructure.
•  Coordinate and supervise contractors for repair or renovation work.
•  Address and resolve maintenance requests and issues promptly.
3. Tenant and Occupant Management
•  Act as the primary point of contact for tenants or building occupants regarding complaints, requests, and inquiries.
•  Ensure tenant satisfaction by resolving issues and maintaining a high standard of building amenities.
•  Facilitate tenant move-ins and move-outs, including inspection and documentation.
4. Safety and Compliance
•  Ensure compliance with local building codes, safety regulations, and environmental standards.
•  Conduct regular fire drills, safety checks, and emergency preparedness exercises.
•  Manage security measures, including surveillance systems and access control.
5. Financial and Administrative Duties
•  Develop and manage the building's operating budget.
•  Track and approve expenditures, ensuring cost-effectiveness.
•  Prepare and present reports on building operations, expenses, and performance.
6. Vendor and Contractor Management
•  Select, negotiate, and manage service contracts for maintenance, cleaning, security, and landscaping.
•  Monitor contractor performance and ensure compliance with agreements.
7. Energy and Sustainability Initiatives
•  Implement energy-saving measures to reduce operational costs and environmental impact.
•  Monitor energy and water consumption and suggest efficiency improvements.
8. Crisis and Emergency Response
•  Coordinate responses to emergencies such as power outages, fires, and natural disasters.
•  Serve as the liaison with emergency services, including police, fire, and medical teams.
9. Space Management and Optimization
•  Plan and manage building spaces for maximum efficiency and functionality.
•  Oversee office layouts, furniture arrangements, and common area utilization.
10. Communication and Reporting
•  Maintain open communication with building owners, tenants, and stakeholders.
•  Provide regular updates and reports on building status, issues, and improvement plans.

Qualification

· Bachelor degree, preferably from technical background (electrical, civil, mechanical engineering, etc)

· Able to work under pressure.

· Able to lead and collaborate effectively in a team.

· Have good analytical sense and problem solver.

· Have good communication skill, to interact with vary stakeholders.

· Attractive and easy-going personality but also could be firm regarding on situation

Related Experience:

Preferably 5 years of working experience in Property & Facilities Management

Management experience required

 

Skills:

· Expertise in property management, building management, tenant relation

· Corporate transportation as support activity of optimal company

· Problem solving

· Demonstrate good leadership and teamwork skills

· Highly committed to quality and timely result of the works

title: Management Trainee
raw_description:
• Manage and ensure the calibration of all measuring and testing equipment is carried out in accordance with established schedules, standards, and applicable regulations.
  • Oversee calibration documentation, certificates, and equipment status to ensure traceability and compliance with the quality management system.
  • Analyze calibration results, address out-of-tolerance equipment, and coordinate corrective actions.
  • Coordinate with relevant departments and external calibration laboratories/vendors to ensure smooth calibration activities.
  • Lead the calibration team, prepare periodic reports, and support internal and external audits related to the calibration system.
raw_requirements:
• Bachelor's degree in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, Food Technology, or other related disciplines.
  • Prior experience in Quality Assurance, Calibration, or Laboratory operations is an advantage.
  • Strong understanding of equipment calibration systems and ISO 22000 and ISO/IEC 17025 standards.
  • Demonstrated leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills, with the ability to coordinate effectively across departments and with external vendors.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office and calibration administration/documentation systems.
  • Willing to be based in Purwakarta.

title: Corp. EHS Manager
location: Jl. Raya Bogor KM 26.6 - Jakarta Timur
raw_description:
• Ensure the implementation of Occupational Health & Safety (OHS) and environmental management programs (water, air, hazardous waste, and energy) across all factory sites in compliance with regulatory requirements.
  • Manage legal compliance aspects and monitor updates to relevant laws and regulations.
  • Develop annual EHS and WWTP roadmaps and targets for each factory.
  • Design and implement training and certification programs for EHS WWTP personnel across all factory sites.
raw_requirements:
• Bachelor’s degree (S1) in Environmental Engineering, Public Health, Chemical Engineering, Food Technology, or a related field.
  • Minimum of 2 years of experience as an EHS Manager in an FMCG company.
  • Strong knowledge of Occupational Health & Safety (OHS), Environmental, and Energy Management Systems.
  • In-depth understanding of industrial wastewater quality standards for the food and beverage industry in compliance with Government and regional regulations.
  • Certified in ISO 18001, ISO 14001, and ISO 50001.
  • Proven experience as a Lead Auditor for SMK3.
  • Willing to travel for business trips to seven factory locations.
